testName: should login with valid credentials to LMS, and load a skill, play, pause, and resume the first video

fix.summary: Don't assert exactly one Close-content-sidebar element or `not.toBeExisting` on a single Close-sidebar label. Use one compound selector and close only when present.
fix.pattern: Remove `expect(closeSidebarButtons).toBeElementsArrayOfSize({ eq: 1 })` and `expect($('[aria-label="Close sidebar"]')).not.toBeExisting()`. Replace with: const closeSidebarButton = await $('[aria-label="Close content sidebar"], [aria-label="Close sidebar"]'); if (await closeSidebarButton.isExisting()) { await closeSidebarButton.waitForClickable({ timeout: 10000 }); await closeSidebarButton.click(); }

investigationFindings: Triage PR #280 / commit 54e72a3: brittle exact-array-size on $$ close buttons. LMS/learn chrome renders 0, 1, or multiple matching nodes depending on viewport, sidebar state, and which inner iframe is active.
rootCauseChain: ASSERTION_MISMATCH -> assumed DOM cardinality -> array-size assertion fails on legitimate variation
